steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank is an incomplete independent clause that ends after the preposition “in the.” After the blank is a complete independent clause: “the Golden Gate Bridge in the United States, at 1,280 meters, is even longer.” These two must be joined.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: When joining two independent clauses with a coordinating conjunction, place a comma before the conjunction. Choice D (“world, but”) correctly positions the comma before “but,” which joins the two clauses with the contrast the sentence requires. The others fail by omitting the comma, adding unwanted punctuation, or leaving out the conjunction. That matches choice D — D is correct.
